Property: Atom‐condensed Fukui functions f⁺ (electrophilic attack), f⁻ (nucleophilic attack), f⁰ (radical attack) and the dual descriptor on neutral aniline; identification of the most electrophilic and most nucleophilic sites.
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier “standard”), exact four-center integrals (density fitting OFF) in PySCF.
Geometry provenance: user-supplied structure /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz; no re-optimization (single-point analysis on that fixed geometry: Fukui//input xyz).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet parent); cation and anion states automatically treated as doublets.
Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (engine subcommand `fukui`, PySCF backend).
